violations.text: The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Staff set-up sanitizer in 3-bay sink during inspection. PIC to call for service New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2024

Sausages were observed held at 128-131 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Staff reheated during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings in the basement near walk-in cooler. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2024
Containers of sliced potatoes and pickles were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2024
Pasta was observed cooling in the 3-bay sink. Staff removed from 3-bay sink during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2024

Reach-in refrigerator and prep unit in kitchen are not in good repair. Refrigerator was observed holding an ambient temperature of 50 degrees (no foods requiring refrigeration stored inside); the foods in the prep unit were observed held on ice and not operating. PIC stated service has been contacted for both units. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2024

The dish machine was not dispensing sanitizer as evidenced by test strip reading 0 ppm. All dishes and utensils must be sanitized in 3-bay sink until machine is serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2024
Al leak was observed at the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2024
